articles.title: Rupee rebounds from all-time low, gains 48 paise to 91.57 against US dollar in early trade

Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are also a significant factor influencing the currency's performance.

articles.title: Crude shock: After 65% annual surge, Iran-Israel war raises margin fears for BPCL, IOC and HPCL. Time to sell?
articles.summary: Indian oil marketing companies (OMCs) like BPCL, IOC, and HPCL have benefited from low crude prices, but rising tensions between Iran, Israel, and the US have pushed crude prices up nearly 30% this year. This surge could squeeze refining margins and hurt OMC profitability. Experts suggest investors sell OMC stocks as crude prices are expected to rise further, negatively impacting these companies.
articles.raw_summary: Oil marketing companies saw significant stock gains from low crude prices. However, escalating Iran-Israel/US tensions have driven crude prices up nearly 30% this year. This surge risks squeezing refining margins and impacting profitability. Experts advise investors to book profits and consider a sell-on-rise strategy as crude prices are expected to move higher, negatively affecting OMC stocks.

articles.title: Recovery Of RERA Compensation Cannot Be Put On Hold Due To Subsequent Vesting Of Land In State: HP High Court
articles.summary: The Himachal Pradesh High Court has held that recovery proceedings initiated pursuant to final order of the Real Estate Regulatory Authority cannot be stalled merely because the project land subsequently vested in the State Government under separate revenue proceedings. The Court further clarified that once a RERA order granting monetary relief attained finality, authorities were bound to enforce it through the statutory recovery mechanisms available under revenue law. Justice Jyotsna Rewal Dua

articles.title: Enforcement, yes — but with accountability: Understanding arrest provisions in GST law
articles.summary: The article explains the arrest provisions in India's GST law, emphasizing that while tax evasion cases justify such measures, arrests should be made with accountability. Former CBIC chairman Najib Shah highlights that legal grounds alone do not mandate arrests, ensuring a balanced approach to enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: Given the large-scale detection of tax evasion cases, which have a multiplier effect on the economy and may even fund anti-national activities, arrest provisions are necessary in fiscal laws. However, the Board’s circular makes it clear that the mere existence of legal grounds for arrest does not automatically mandate an arrest, writes former CBIC chairman Najib Shah.

articles.title: India's balance of payments outlook faces risks from West Asia crisis
articles.summary: India's external sector outlook remains broadly manageable but faces rising risks from geopolitical developments in West Asia that could push up crude oil prices and widen the current account deficit (CAD), according to a recent research note by Yes Bank. The report noted that India recorded a Balance of Payments (BoP) deficit of USD 24.4 billion in Q3 FY26, wider than the USD 10.9 billion deficit in the previous quarter, largely due to capital account outflows.
